title: Bone Broth Chicken Soup
description: Nourishing chicken soup made with homemade-style bone broth, tender chicken, and plenty of vegetables. Healing comfort in a bowl.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or soup pot over medium heat. Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
Add chicken to pot and brown on both sides, about 4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté for 5-6 minutes until starting to soften.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in bone broth, add thyme sprigs and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
Return chicken to the pot. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25-30 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken and shred with two forks. Discard thyme stems and bay leaves.
Return shredded chicken to the soup. Stir in lemon juice and parsley.
Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve hot.

title: Grilled Chicken Salad with Ranch
description: A satisfying main-dish salad with juicy grilled chicken, crisp vegetables, and creamy paleo ranch dressing.
instructions: For ranch dressing: Whisk together mayo, coconut milk, dill, chives, garlic, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Season chicken breasts with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Grill chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Let chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
Arrange chopped romaine on a large platter or divide among plates.
Top with sliced chicken, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, avocado, and hard-boiled eggs.
Drizzle with ranch dressing and serve immediately.

title: Creamy Tomato Basil Soup
description: Velvety smooth tomato soup with fresh basil and a touch of coconut cream. Pure comfort without the dairy.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion and sauté for 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 25 minutes.
Remove from heat. Add most of the fresh basil (reserve some for garnish).
Use an immersion blender to puree until completely smooth (or blend in batches in a regular blender).
Stir in coconut cream and balsamic vinegar. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve topped with remaining fresh basil.

title: Hearty Beef & Root Vegetable Stew
description: A rich, slow-simmered beef stew loaded with root vegetables and fresh herbs. Classic comfort food, completely dairy-free.
instructions: Pat beef cubes dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown beef in batches, getting a deep sear on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per batch. Don't crowd the pot. Set browned beef aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion and celery, cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and tomato paste. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until paste darkens slightly.
Pour in bone broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Return beef to the pot.
Add rosemary, thyme, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Add carrots, parsnips, and sweet potato. Cover and continue simmering for 45-60 minutes until beef is fork-tender and vegetables are cooked through.
Remove herb sprigs and bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve topped with fresh parsley.

title: Roasted Butternut Squash Soup
description: Silky smooth butternut squash soup with warm spices and coconut cream. Deep caramelized flavor from roasting the squash first.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place squash halves cut side up on the baking sheet. Add onion quarters and garlic cloves. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Roast for 40-45 minutes until squash is deeply caramelized and fork-tender.
Scoop squash flesh from the skin. Squeeze garlic cloves from their skins. Transfer squash, garlic, and onion to a large pot.
Add bone broth, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cumin. Bring to a simmer.
Blend with an immersion blender until completely smooth. Stir in coconut cream.
Simmer for 5 minutes to meld flavors. Taste and adjust seasonings.
Serve topped with toasted pumpkin seeds and a drizzle of coconut cream.

title: Italian Wedding Soup
description: Light yet satisfying broth soup with mini turkey meatballs, leafy greens, and vegetables. A clean take on the Italian classic.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: 2 minced for meatballs, 2 minced for soup

instructions: Make meatballs: Combine ground turkey, egg, almond flour, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 tsp Italian seasoning, and a pinch of salt in a bowl. Mix gently. Roll into 1-inch balls (about 30 meatballs).
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 5 minutes until starting to soften.
Add remaining garlic and Italian seasoning. Cook 1 minute.
Pour in bone broth and bring to a boil.
Gently drop meatballs into the simmering broth. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for 10-12 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
Add escarole or spinach and cook for 2-3 minutes until wilted.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Taste and adjust.
Serve topped with fresh parsley.
